content.problemStatement:
The elections in Chefland have concluded, and the results are in!

Chef received XXX votes, and his rival Chefu received YYY.

Chef thinks he dominated the election if and only if he received **at least double** the number of votes Chefu received.

Did Chef dominate the election?


Input Format

The only line of input contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the number of votes received by Chef and Chefu, respectively.


Output Format
Print a single line containing the answer: either `"Yes"` or `"No"` (without quotes), depending on whether Chef dominated the election or not.


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `No`, `no`, `NO`, and `nO` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the votes received by Chef and Chefu respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing "Yes" if Chef received at least double the votes of Chefu, otherwise print "No". Letter case does not matter.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each case, compare X with 2*Y. If X >= 2*Y output "Yes", else output "No". The operation is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time check per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        Y = int(next(it))
        out.append('Yes' if X >= 2 * Y else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
